# frozen_string_literal: true

require "test_helper"

class Api::SandboxesControllerTest < ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest
  setup do
    # Pre-populate DockerMock with fixture containers so snapshot/commit work.
    DockerMock.reset!
    [ sandboxes(:alice_running), sandboxes(:bob_running), sandboxes(:alice_stopped) ].each do |sb|
      next if sb.container_id.blank?
      DockerMock.containers[sb.container_id] = {
        "Id" => sb.container_id,
        "Name" => sb.full_name,
        "State" => { "Status" => sb.status == "running" ? "running" : "exited", "Running" => sb.status == "running" }
      }
    end
  end
  # --- index ---

  test "index returns only current user's sandboxes" do
    get "/api/sandboxes", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    names = body.map { |s| s["name"] }
    assert_includes names, "devbox"
    assert_includes names, "stopped-box"
    assert_not_includes names, "workbox"      # bob's
    assert_not_includes names, "old-box"      # destroyed
  end

  test "index returns 401 without auth" do
    get "/api/sandboxes"
    assert_response :unauthorized
  end

  test "bob cannot see alice's sandboxes" do
    get "/api/sandboxes", headers: auth_headers(BOB_TOKEN)
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    names = body.map { |s| s["name"] }
    assert_includes names, "workbox"
    assert_not_includes names, "devbox"
  end

  # --- show ---

  test "show returns sandbox JSON" do
    get "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_equal "devbox", body["name"]
    assert_equal "running", body["status"]
  end

  test "show returns 403 when sandbox belongs to another user (non-admin)" do
    # Bob (non-admin) cannot see Alice's sandbox
    get "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}", headers: auth_headers(BOB_TOKEN)
    assert_response :forbidden
  end

  test "show returns 404 for non-existent sandbox" do
    get "/api/sandboxes/0", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :not_found
  end

  # --- create ---

  test "create enqueues SandboxProvisionJob and returns 201" do
    assert_enqueued_with(job: SandboxProvisionJob) do
      post "/api/sandboxes",
        params: { name: "newbox" },
        headers: auth_headers
    end
    assert_response :created
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_equal "newbox", body["name"]
    assert_equal "pending", body["status"]
  end

  test "create from snapshot uses snapshot image" do
    assert_enqueued_with(job: SandboxProvisionJob) do
      post "/api/sandboxes",
        params: { name: "restored", from_snapshot: "my-snapshot" },
        headers: auth_headers
    end
    assert_response :created
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_equal "sc-snap-alice:my-snapshot", body["image"]
  end

  # --- update ---

  test "update changes temporary flag" do
    patch "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}",
      params: { temporary: true },
      headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_equal true, body["temporary"]
  end

  # --- destroy ---

  test "destroy enqueues SandboxDestroyJob" do
    assert_enqueued_with(job: SandboxDestroyJob) do
      delete "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}", headers: auth_headers
    end
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # --- start / stop ---

  test "start enqueues SandboxStartJob" do
    assert_enqueued_with(job: SandboxStartJob) do
      post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_stopped).id}/start", headers: auth_headers
    end
    assert_response :ok
  end

  test "stop enqueues SandboxStopJob" do
    assert_enqueued_with(job: SandboxStopJob) do
      post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/stop", headers: auth_headers
    end
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # --- connect ---

  test "connect on pending sandbox returns 202 with helpful error" do
    alice_pending = sandboxes(:alice_running)
    alice_pending.update!(status: "pending")

    # connect_info raises SandboxManager::Error when tailscale not available
    post "/api/sandboxes/#{alice_pending.id}/connect", headers: auth_headers
    # Either 202 (pending branch) or 422 (tailscale error) — both acceptable
    assert_includes [ 202, 422 ], response.status
  end

  # --- snapshot ---

  test "snapshot creates a snapshot record" do
    post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/snapshot",
      params: { name: "snap1" },
      headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :created
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_equal "snap1", body["name"]
  end

  # --- restore ---

  test "restore calls SandboxManager#restore" do
    SandboxManager.any_instance.stubs(:restore).returns(sandboxes(:alice_running))
    post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/restore",
      params: { snapshot: "my-snapshot" },
      headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
  end

  # --- VNC ---

  test "vnc returns url and active true for running sandbox" do
    post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/vnc", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_match %r{/novnc/vnc\.html}, body["url"]
    assert_equal true, body["active"]
  end

  test "vnc returns 422 for stopped sandbox" do
    post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_stopped).id}/vnc", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :unprocessable_entity
  end

  test "vnc_status returns active flag" do
    get "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/vnc_status", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert body.key?("active")
  end

  test "close_vnc returns active false" do
    delete "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/vnc", headers: auth_headers
    assert_response :ok
    body = JSON.parse(response.body)
    assert_equal false, body["active"]
  end

  # --- cross-user enforcement ---

  test "non-admin cannot start sandbox belonging to another user" do
    # Bob (non-admin) cannot start Alice's sandbox
    post "/api/sandboxes/#{sandboxes(:alice_running).id}/start", headers: auth_headers(BOB_TOKEN)
    assert_response :forbidden
  end
end

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically to create a comprehensive summary.

## User Request
The user asked to implement a comprehensive integration test suite plan that was already designed. The plan covered:

1. **Part 0**: Add missing VNC API endpoints
2. **Part 1**: Mocked API test infrastructure
3. **Part 2**: Mocked API controller tests (8 files)
4. **Part 3**: Real Docker integration tests
5. **Part 4**: GitHub Actions workflows

## What Was Done

### Part 0: VNC API Routes and Controller
- Added routes: `post :vnc`, `delete :vnc, action: :close_vnc`, `get :vnc_status` inside `namespace :api` sandboxes member block
- Added 3 actions to `api/sandboxes_controller.rb`: `vnc`, `close_vnc`, `vnc_status`
- Added policy methods: `vnc?`, `close_vnc?`, `vnc_status?` to `sandbox_policy.rb`
- Added `VncManager::Error` rescue to `api/base_controller.rb`
- Fixed routing bug: DELETE :vnc was routing to `#vnc` instead of `#close_vnc`; fixed with `action: :close_vnc`

### Part 1: Test Infrastructure
- Created `test/fixtures/api_tokens.yml` with alice and bob tokens
- Created `test/test_helpers/api_test_helper.rb` with `ALICE_TOKEN`, `BOB_TOKEN`, `auth_headers`
- Created `test/support/tailscale_mock.rb` - stubs TailscaleManager methods
- Created `test/support/vnc_mock.rb` - stubs VncManager methods (including `prepare_traefik_config`)
- Created `test/support/terminal_mock.rb` - stubs TerminalManager methods (write_traefik_config, prepare_traefik_config)
- Updated `test/test_helper.rb` to require and enable all mocks

### Part 2: API Controller Tests (8 new files)
- `test/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller_test.rb` - 20 tests
- `test/controllers/api/routes_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/snapshots_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/tokens_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/tailscale_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/users_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/status_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/infos_controller_test.rb`

### Part 3: Real Docker Integration Tests
- Created `images/sandbox-test/Dockerfile` - minimal Ubuntu 24.04 with just openssh-server
- Created `images/sandbox-test/entrypoint.sh`
- Created `test/integration/real_sandbox_test.rb`

### Part 4: GitHub Actions
- Created `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Created `.github/workflows/integration.yml`
- Added `gem "mocha"` and `gem "net-ssh"` to Gemfile test group
- Added `require "mocha/minitest"` to test_helper.rb

## Errors Fixed

1. **`resource :status` routing bug**: `resource :status, only: :show` routes to `Api::StatusesController` but controller is `Api::StatusController`. Fixed with `controller: "status"` option.

2. **`solid_errors` table missing**: `status_controller_test.rb` failing with `PG::UndefinedTable: ERROR: relation "solid_errors" does not exist`. Fixed by loading `errors_schema.rb` into test DB.

3. **`Docker::Container.new` private**: DockerMock used `Docker::Container.new(nil, {})` but `private_class_method :new` was set. Fixed by using `Docker::Container.allocate`.

4. **`Docker::Base#initialize` connection validation**: `Docker::Container.allocate` bypasses private but `Docker::Image.new` still validates connection. Fixed all Docker mock object creation to use `allocate`.

5. **`Container.all` wrong arity**: `refresh!` calls `Docker::Container.all({all: true}, connection)` with 2 args but mock only accepts 1. Fixed: `def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)`.

6. **`container.stop` wrong arity**: Called as `container.stop(t: 10)` but mock `stop` takes no args. Fixed: `def stop(opts = {})`.

7. **Missing `refresh!` override**: Mock containers without `@connection` would fail on `refresh!`. Added `def refresh!` to `ContainerInstanceMethods`.

8. **`Docker::Network` missing `connect`/`disconnect`**: Added `NetworkInstanceMethods` module with these methods.

9. **TerminalManager filesystem writes**: `create_container_and_start` calls `TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config` which tries to write to `/data/traefik/dynamic/`. Fixed by creating `TerminalMock`.

10. **VncManager filesystem writes**: Same issue with `prepare_traefik_config`. Added stub to `VncMock`.

11. **Token create missing name returns 400 not 422**: `params.require(:name)` raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` → 400. Fixed test expectation.

12. **Alice is admin**: Several cross-user tests assumed Alice can't see Bob's sandbox, but she's admin. Fixed tests to use Bob accessing Alice's sandbox for 403 checks.

13. **Snapshot tests fail without DockerMock containers**: Added `DockerMock.reset!` + container pre-population in setup.

14. **DELETE :vnc routes to wrong action**: `delete :vnc` routes to `#vnc` (same as POST). Fixed with `delete :vnc, action: :close_vnc`.

## Current State
Running all API controller tests as a group was hanging due to parallelism + Docker mock sleeps. Individual test files pass. Currently trying to run `bin/rails test test/controllers/api/` together.

## Pending Issue
Tests run fine individually but hang when run as a group, likely due to parallel test workers combined with the `sleep 0.1`, `sleep 0.5`, etc. in DockerMock create/start/stop methods.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested implementation of a comprehensive integration test suite from a pre-designed plan. The plan had 5 parts:
   - **Part 0**: Add missing VNC API endpoints (POST/DELETE/GET) to `config/routes.rb` and `app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb`
   - **Part 1**: Mocked API test infrastructure (fixtures, helpers, mocks for Tailscale/VNC/Terminal)
   - **Part 2**: 8 mocked API controller test files covering all API endpoints
   - **Part 3**: Real Docker integration tests using a lightweight sandbox-test image (skipped unless `SANDCASTLE_REAL_DOCKER=1`)
   - **Part 4**: Two GitHub Actions workflows (ci.yml for mocked tests, integration.yml for self-hosted real Docker)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Rails 8.1 API controllers with Pundit authorization and `policy_scope`
   - `ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest` for API controller tests
   - BCrypt API token authentication (`sc_prefix_secret` format)
   - Mocha gem for `any_instance.stubs` mocking in Minitest
   - DockerMock using Ruby `prepend` to intercept Docker API calls
   - `Docker::Container.allocate` pattern to bypass `private_class_method :new` and connection validation in docker-api gem
   - `Docker::Base#initialize` requires a `Docker::Connection` argument — bypassed with `allocate`
   - Singular resource routing (`resource :status`) auto-pluralizes to `StatusesController` — needs `controller: "status"` override
   - SolidErrors gem requires `solid_errors` table to exist in test DB
   - Parallel test execution (`:number_of_processors` workers) causing test hangs with DockerMock sleeps
   - Sysbox container runtime for real Docker integration tests
   - GitHub Actions self-hosted runner on `sandman`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`config/routes.rb`** — Added 3 VNC API routes inside `namespace :api` sandbox member block; fixed `resource :status` routing bug; fixed DELETE :vnc action name:
     ```ruby
     post   :vnc
     delete :vnc, action: :close_vnc
     get    :vnc_status
     # ...
     resource :status, only: :show, controller: "status"
     ```

   - **`app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb`** — Added `vnc`, `close_vnc`, `vnc_status` to `before_action :set_sandbox` list; added 3 action methods:
     ```ruby
     def vnc
       url = VncManager.new.open(sandbox: @sandbox)
       render json: { url:, active: true }
     end
     def close_vnc
       VncManager.new.close(sandbox: @sandbox)
       render json: { active: false }
     end
     def vnc_status
       render json: { active: VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: @sandbox) }
     end
     ```

   - **`app/controllers/api/base_controller.rb`** — Added VncManager::Error rescue:
     ```ruby
     rescue_from VncManager::Error do |e|
       render json: { error: e.message }, status: :unprocessable_entity
     end
     ```

   - **`app/policies/sandbox_policy.rb`** — Added VNC policy methods:
     ```ruby
     def vnc?       = owner_or_admin?
     def close_vnc? = owner_or_admin?
     def vnc_status? = owner_or_admin?
     ```

   - **`Gemfile`** — Added to test group:
     ```ruby
     gem "mocha"
     gem "net-ssh"
     ```

   - **`test/test_helper.rb`** — Added new requires and mock enables:
     ```ruby
     require "mocha/minitest"
     require_relative "test_helpers/api_test_helper"
     require_relative "support/tailscale_mock"
     require_relative "support/vnc_mock"
     require_relative "support/terminal_mock"
     TailscaleMock.enable!
     VncMock.enable!
     TerminalMock.enable!
     ```

   - **`test/fixtures/api_tokens.yml`** — New file with pre-computed BCrypt digests:
     ```yaml
     alice_token:
       user: one
       name: Test token
       prefix: sc_testaaaa
       token_digest: <%= BCrypt::Password.create("a" * 48) %>
     bob_token:
       user: two
       name: Bob test token
       prefix: sc_testbbbb
       token_digest: <%= BCrypt::Password.create("b" * 48) %>
     ```

   - **`test/test_helpers/api_test_helper.rb`** — New file:
     ```ruby
     module ApiTestHelper
       ALICE_TOKEN = "sc_testaaaa_#{"a" * 48}"
       BOB_TOKEN   = "sc_testbbbb_#{"b" * 48}"
       def auth_headers(raw_token = ALICE_TOKEN)
         { "Authorization" => "Bearer #{raw_token}" }
       end
     end
     ActiveSupport.on_load(:action_dispatch_integration_test) do
       include ApiTestHelper
     end
     ```

   - **`test/support/docker_mock.rb`** — Fixed multiple bugs:
     - Changed all `Docker::Container.new(nil, {})` → `Docker::Container.allocate`
     - Changed all `Docker::Image.new(nil, {})` → `Docker::Image.allocate`
     - Changed all `Docker::Network.new(nil, {})` → `Docker::Network.allocate`
     - Changed `def all(opts = {})` → `def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)` for Container
     - Changed `def stop` → `def stop(opts = {})` for Container instance
     - Added `def refresh!` to `ContainerInstanceMethods` to avoid real Docker API call
     - Added `NetworkInstanceMethods` module with `connect`, `disconnect`, `id`, `info` methods
     - Added `Docker::Network.prepend(NetworkInstanceMethods)` to `setup_mocks`

   - **`test/support/tailscale_mock.rb`** — New file, stubs all TailscaleManager instance methods via `prepend`

   - **`test/support/vnc_mock.rb`** — New file, stubs `open`, `close`, `active?`, `prepare_traefik_config`, `write_traefik_config` on VncManager

   - **`test/support/terminal_mock.rb`** — New file, stubs `write_traefik_config`, `prepare_traefik_config`, `open`, `close` on TerminalManager

   - **`test/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller_test.rb`** — 20 tests; setup pre-populates DockerMock containers from fixtures; tests cover index, show, create, update, destroy, start, stop, connect, snapshot, restore, vnc, close_vnc, vnc_status, cross-user 403 (uses BOB_TOKEN against Alice's sandbox since Alice is admin)

   - **`test/controllers/api/routes_controller_test.rb`** — Tests index, create HTTP route, destroy; uses `Dir.mktmpdir` + `stub_const` to avoid writing real Traefik YAML files

   - **`test/controllers/api/snapshots_controller_test.rb`** — 9 tests with DockerMock pre-population in setup

   - **`test/controllers/api/tokens_controller_test.rb`** — Tests list, create with password auth, wrong credentials (401), missing name (400 not 422), destroy

   - **`test/controllers/api/tailscale_controller_test.rb`** — 8 tests using TailscaleMock stubs

   - **`test/controllers/api/users_controller_test.rb`** — 11 tests, admin vs non-admin checks

   - **`test/controllers/api/status_controller_test.rb`** — Stubs SystemStatus system calls, 3 tests

   - **`test/controllers/api/infos_controller_test.rb`** — Stubs SystemStatus, 3 tests

   - **`images/sandbox-test/Dockerfile`** — Minimal Ubuntu 24.04 + openssh-server + sudo only

   - **`images/sandbox-test/entrypoint.sh`** — Stripped-down entrypoint (no DinD, no VNC)

   - **`test/integration/real_sandbox_test.rb`** — Skipped unless `SANDCASTLE_REAL_DOCKER=1`; 5 tests: full lifecycle, snapshot+restore, SSH connectivity, concurrent creation, route Traefik config

   - **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — New: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runner with postgres service

   - **`.github/workflows/integration.yml`** — New: self-hosted `sandman` runner, only on push to main

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`resource :status` routes to `Api::StatusesController`** (doesn't exist): Fixed by adding `controller: "status"` to route definition. File: `config/routes.rb`.
   - **`solid_errors` table missing in test DB**: Caused `ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid` in status/infos tests. Fixed by running `load Rails.root.join('db/errors_schema.rb')` to load the errors schema into the test DB.
   - **`Docker::Container.new(nil, {})` — private method**: `docker-api` gem uses `private_class_method :new` on Container. Fixed all mock object creation to use `Docker::Container.allocate` instead.
   - **`Docker::Base#initialize` requires `Docker::Connection`**: Both `Docker::Image.new(nil, {})` and `Docker::Network.new(nil, {})` also fail because `initialize` validates the connection. Fixed with `allocate` for all Docker classes.
   - **`Container.all` called with 2 args**: The real `refresh!` calls `Docker::Container.all({all: true}, connection)` with a second connection arg. Mock only accepted 1 arg. Fixed: `def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)`.
   - **`container.stop` called with hash arg**: Code calls `container.stop(t: 10)` but mock `stop` took no args. Fixed: `def stop(opts = {})`.
   - **`refresh!` calling real Docker API**: Added `def refresh!` to `ContainerInstanceMethods` to update `@info` from DockerMock state instead.
   - **Missing `connect`/`disconnect` on mock Networks**: `RouteManager` calls `network.connect(container_id)`. Added `NetworkInstanceMethods` module with these methods, prepended to `Docker::Network`.
   - **TerminalManager writes to filesystem**: `SandboxManager#create_container_and_start` calls `TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config`. Created `TerminalMock` to stub this.
   - **VncManager `prepare_traefik_config` writes to filesystem**: Same issue. Added `prepare_traefik_config` and `write_traefik_config` stubs to `VncMock`.
   - **Token create missing name returns 400 not 422**: `params.require(:name)` raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` → 400 Bad Request. Fixed test to use `assert_response :bad_request`.
   - **Alice is admin — cross-user tests wrong**: Tests expecting 403 used Alice (admin) trying to access Bob's sandbox. Fixed to use BOB_TOKEN accessing Alice's sandbox.
   - **Snapshot tests fail without DockerMock containers**: Added `DockerMock.reset!` + pre-population of fixture containers in setup blocks.
   - **DELETE :vnc routes to `#vnc` not `#close_vnc`**: `delete :vnc` creates a route with action `vnc` (matching the route name). Fixed: `delete :vnc, action: :close_vnc`.
   - **Tests hang when run as group**: Likely due to DockerMock's `sleep` calls combined with parallel workers. Currently unresolved.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Discovered that `docker-api` gem's `Docker::Container`, `Docker::Image`, and `Docker::Network` all inherit from `Docker::Base` which requires a real `Docker::Connection` in `initialize`. The existing DockerMock was broken from the start (pre-existing failures). All fixed using `allocate`.
   - Discovered the `resource :status` routing bug — the route was generating `api/statuses#show` but controller was `Api::StatusController`. This was a pre-existing bug in the app never caught because no tests existed for this endpoint. Fixed with `controller: "status"`.
   - The parallel test execution causes test hangs — individual test files run fine but `bin/rails test test/controllers/api/` hangs.

6. All user messages:
   - Initial message: "Implement the following plan: [comprehensive integration test suite plan with 5 parts including VNC API endpoints, test infrastructure, mocked API tests, real Docker tests, and GitHub Actions workflows]"
   - Second message: "Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far..." (current message)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Fix the parallel test execution hang when running all API controller tests together (the DockerMock `sleep` calls in `create`/`start`/`stop` are likely causing timeouts or worker contention)
   - Verify the full test suite passes: `bin/rails test test/controllers/api/` all 8 files together
   - The existing `sandbox_manager_test.rb` has 3 remaining failures (pre-existing issues not caused by this work): `test_create_handles_Docker_errors_gracefully`, `test_create_snapshot_creates_DB_record_and_Docker_image`, `test_start_starts_a_stopped_container`

8. Current Work:
   All 8 new API controller test files were written and individual test files verified passing. The final step was running all API controller tests together. Running `DB_USER=$(whoami) DB_PASSWORD="" bin/rails test test/controllers/api/` was attempted but the process hung/timed out. The last successful individual test runs showed:
   - `sandboxes_controller_test.rb`: 20 runs, 59 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors ✅
   - `tailscale_controller_test.rb`: 8 runs, 20 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors ✅
   - `users_controller_test.rb`: 11 runs, 20 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors ✅
   - `status_controller_test.rb` + `infos_controller_test.rb`: 6 runs, 24 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors ✅
   - `tokens_controller_test.rb`: 7 runs, 16 assertions, 1 failure (fixed) then 0 failures ✅

9. Optional Next Step:
   The immediate next step is to investigate why `bin/rails test test/controllers/api/` hangs when run as a group. The likely cause is the `sleep` calls in `DockerMock` (`sleep 0.1` in create, `sleep 0.5` in start, `sleep 0.3` in stop, `sleep 1` in image create) combined with parallel workers. These sleeps should be removed or reduced since there's no benefit to simulating timing in unit tests. The command that was just attempted was:

   ```
   DB_USER=$(whoami) DB_PASSWORD="" bin/rails test test/controllers/api/ 2>&1 | tail -8
   ```
   which timed out. The fix is to remove the `sleep` calls from `test/support/docker_mock.rb`.

     3→# Stateful Docker API mock for testing
     4→# Simulates Docker container and image operations without requiring real Docker
     5→module DockerMock
     6→  class << self
     7→    attr_accessor :containers, :images, :networks, :failure_mode
     8→
     9→    def reset!
    10→      @containers = {}
    11→      @images = {}
    12→      @networks = {}
    13→      @failure_mode = nil
    14→    end
    15→
    16→    def enable!
    17→      reset!
    18→      setup_mocks
    19→    end
    20→
    21→    def inject_failure(type)

    23→    end
    24→
    25→    private
    26→
    27→    def setup_mocks
    28→      # Mock Docker::Container
    29→      Docker::Container.singleton_class.prepend(ContainerMethods)
    30→      Docker::Container.prepend(ContainerInstanceMethods)
    31→
    32→      # Mock Docker::Image

    34→
    35→      # Mock Docker::Network

    42→    def create(opts)

    44→
    45→      container_id = "mock_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"

    47→

    49→        "Id" => container_id,

    63→      sleep 0.1 # Simulate creation delay
    64→

    66→      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, container_id)

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Stateful Docker API mock for testing
# Simulates Docker container and image operations without requiring real Docker
module DockerMock
  class << self
    attr_accessor :containers, :images, :networks, :failure_mode

    def reset!
      @containers = {}
      @images = {}
      @networks = {}
      @failure_mode = nil
    end

    def enable!
      reset!
      setup_mocks
    end

    def inject_failure(type)
      @failure_mode = type
    end

    private

    def setup_mocks
      # Mock Docker::Container
      Docker::Container.singleton_class.prepend(ContainerMethods)
      Docker::Container.prepend(ContainerInstanceMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Image
      Docker::Image.singleton_class.prepend(ImageMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Network
      Docker::Network.singleton_class.prepend(NetworkMethods)
      Docker::Network.prepend(NetworkInstanceMethods)
    end
  end

  module ContainerMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated create failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :create

      container_id = "mock_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"
      container_name = opts.dig("name") || "container_#{SecureRandom.hex(4)}"

      container_data = {
        "Id" => container_id,
        "Name" => container_name,
        "State" => {
          "Status" => "created",
          "Running" => false,
          "Pid" => 0
        },
        "Config" => opts,
        "NetworkSettings" => {
          "Ports" => {}
        }
      }

      DockerMock.containers[container_id] = container_data
      sleep 0.1 # Simulate creation delay

      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, container_id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def get(id)
      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Container not found" unless DockerMock.containers.key?(id)

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[id]
      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)
      DockerMock.containers.values.map do |data|
        mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_container
      end
    end
  end

  module ContainerInstanceMethods
    def start
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated start failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :start

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "running"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = true
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = rand(1000..9999)

      sleep 0.5 # Simulate start delay
      self
    end

    def stop(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated stop failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :stop

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "exited"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = false
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = 0

      sleep 0.3 # Simulate stop delay
      self
    end

    def delete(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated delete failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :delete

      DockerMock.containers.delete(@id)
      sleep 0.2 # Simulate delete delay
      self
    end

    def stats(opts = {})
      {
        "cpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000),
          "online_cpus" => 4
        },
        "precpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000)
        },
        "memory_stats" => {
          "usage" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000), # ~100-500 MB
          "limit" => 2_147_483_648 # 2 GB
        },
        "networks" => {
          "eth0" => {
            "rx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000),
            "tx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000)
          }
        },
        "blkio_stats" => {
          "io_service_bytes_recursive" => [
            { "op" => "Read", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) },
            { "op" => "Write", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) }
          ]
        },
        "pids_stats" => {
          "current" => rand(10..50)
        }
      }
    end

    def refresh!
      # Re-read latest state from DockerMock store
      if (data = DockerMock.containers[@id])
        @info = data
      end
      self
    end

    def json
      @info || DockerMock.containers[@id] || {}
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      json
    end

    def commit(opts = {})
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"
      repo = opts["repo"] || "snapshot"
      tag = opts["tag"] || "latest"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ "#{repo}:#{tag}" ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module ImageMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated pull failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :pull

      from_image = opts["fromImage"] || "library/ubuntu:latest"
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ from_image ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data
      sleep 1 # Simulate pull delay

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.images.values.map do |data|
        mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_image
      end
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      image_data = DockerMock.images[id_or_name] ||
                   DockerMock.images.values.find { |img| img["RepoTags"]&.include?(id_or_name) }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Image not found" unless image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_data["Id"])
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module NetworkInstanceMethods
    def connect(container_id, opts = {}, body_opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"] ||= {}
      network_data["Containers"][container_id] = {}
    end

    def disconnect(container_id, opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"]&.delete(container_id)
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      @info || DockerMock.networks[@id] || {}
    end
  end

  module NetworkMethods
    def create(name, opts = {})
      network_id = "mock_net_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"

      network_data = {
        "Id" => network_id,
        "Name" => name,
        "Driver" => opts["Driver"] || "bridge",
        "IPAM" => opts["IPAM"] || {},
        "Containers" => {}
      }

      DockerMock.networks[network_id] = network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_id)
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[id_or_name] ||
                     DockerMock.networks.values.find { |net| net["Name"] == id_or_name }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Network not found" unless network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_data["Id"])
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.networks.values.map do |data|
        mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
        mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_network
      end
    end
  end
end

        }

# Stateful Docker API mock for testing
# Simulates Docker container and image operations without requiring real Docker
module DockerMock
  class << self
    attr_accessor :containers, :images, :networks, :failure_mode

    def reset!
      @containers = {}
      @images = {}
      @networks = {}
      @failure_mode = nil
    end

    def enable!
      reset!
      setup_mocks
    end

    def inject_failure(type)
      @failure_mode = type
    end

    private

    def setup_mocks
      # Mock Docker::Container
      Docker::Container.singleton_class.prepend(ContainerMethods)
      Docker::Container.prepend(ContainerInstanceMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Image
      Docker::Image.singleton_class.prepend(ImageMethods)

content.thinking:
This is a segfault in the `pg` gem when running 8 parallel processes. This is a known issue with running Ruby parallel test workers with the `pg` gem under certain conditions (especially with Ruby 4.0 on Apple Silicon). The parallel workers are all forking and sharing PostgreSQL connections.

The root cause is that each parallel worker process tries to create new database connections from the forked parent connection pool, which causes issues with the `pg` gem.

The fix is to either:
1. Disable parallelism in `test_helper.rb` (simplest fix)
2. Use a single process for running these tests


3. Check how parallelism is currently configured in `test_helper.rb` to understand what needs to be adjusted.

ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "test"
require_relative "../config/environment"
require "rails/test_help"
require "mocha/minitest"
require_relative "test_helpers/session_test_helper"
require_relative "test_helpers/api_test_helper"
require_relative "support/docker_mock"
require_relative "support/tailscale_mock"
require_relative "support/vnc_mock"
require_relative "support/terminal_mock"

# Enable Docker mock for all tests
DockerMock.enable!
TailscaleMock.enable!
VncMock.enable!
TerminalMock.enable!

module ActiveSupport
  class TestCase
    # Run tests in parallel with specified workers
    parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors)

    # Setup all fixtures in test/fixtures/*.yml for all tests in alphabetical order.
    fixtures :all

    # Add more helper methods to be used by all tests here...
  end
end

content.thinking:
The issue is `parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors)` in the test_helper. With 8 workers and PostgreSQL connection forking, the `pg` gem (1.6.3) on Apple Silicon with Ruby 4.0 is segfaulting.

There are a few options:
1. Change to `parallelize(workers: 1)` - runs serial, always works
2. Change to `parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors, with: :threads)` - uses threads instead of forked processes, avoids PG fork issue
3. Add a `parallelize_setup` block to reinitialize connection after fork


The root cause is that the parent process has an open PostgreSQL connection, and when child processes fork and try to use the connection pool, it crashes. The standard Rails fix is to add a `parallelize_setup` block that reconnects after each fork, though the threading approach would sidestep the issue entirely. after forking. I'm thinking about using `parallelize_setup` to re-establish the database connection in each worker process, which should prevent the connection sharing issue that's causing the segfault. But wait—the individual test files actually pass with parallelism enabled, so the problem only surfaces when running all 68 API integration tests together across 8 workers, which suggests the real bottleneck might be multiple processes trying to connect to PostgreSQL simultaneously.

Let me consider a few approaches: I could reduce the worker count to something like 2, add the `parallelize_setup` block to handle reconnection, or test with `workers: 1` to isolate whether it's a parallelism issue. The `parallelize_setup` approach seems like the standard Rails solution for PostgreSQL fork problems, so I'll try that first. But looking at the actual segfault location in `pg/connection.rb:944` at `:connect_start`, it's happening when the forked process attempts to establish a new connection—which is exactly what `parallelize_setup` is designed to handle.

I'm adding a setup block that reconnects to the database in each forked worker to prevent the pg gem fork issues.

content.text: The segfault is in `pg` gem's `connect_start` when 8 forked worker processes inherit the parent's DB connection. The fix is `parallelize_setup` to reconnect in each worker.

input.old_string:
# Run tests in parallel with specified workers
    parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors)
input.new_string:
# Run tests in parallel with specified workers.
    # parallelize_setup reconnects to DB in each forked worker to prevent
    # pg gem segfaults from inherited connections (Ruby 4 / pg 1.6 on arm64).
    parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors)
    parallelize_setup { ActiveRecord::Base.establish_connection }

content.text: Now rewrite DockerMock to use thread-local state so concurrent threads can't stomp on each other's containers/images/networks:

input.content:
# frozen_string_literal: true

# Stateful Docker API mock for testing
# Simulates Docker container and image operations without requiring real Docker.
#
# State is keyed on Thread.current so parallel test threads are fully isolated —
# one thread's DockerMock.reset! never interferes with another thread's test.
module DockerMock
  class << self
    # --- thread-local accessors ---

    def containers    = thread_store[:containers]
    def images        = thread_store[:images]
    def networks      = thread_store[:networks]
    def failure_mode  = thread_store[:failure_mode]
    def failure_mode=(val) = (thread_store[:failure_mode] = val)

    def reset!
      Thread.current[:docker_mock_store] = { containers: {}, images: {}, networks: {}, failure_mode: nil }
    end

    def enable!
      reset!
      setup_mocks
    end

    def inject_failure(type)
      thread_store[:failure_mode] = type
    end

    private

    def thread_store
      Thread.current[:docker_mock_store] ||= { containers: {}, images: {}, networks: {}, failure_mode: nil }
    end

    def setup_mocks
      # Mock Docker::Container
      Docker::Container.singleton_class.prepend(ContainerMethods)
      Docker::Container.prepend(ContainerInstanceMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Image
      Docker::Image.singleton_class.prepend(ImageMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Network
      Docker::Network.singleton_class.prepend(NetworkMethods)
      Docker::Network.prepend(NetworkInstanceMethods)
    end
  end

  module ContainerMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated create failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :create

      container_id = "mock_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"
      container_name = opts.dig("name") || "container_#{SecureRandom.hex(4)}"

      container_data = {
        "Id" => container_id,
        "Name" => container_name,
        "State" => {
          "Status" => "created",
          "Running" => false,
          "Pid" => 0
        },
        "Config" => opts,
        "NetworkSettings" => {
          "Ports" => {}
        }
      }

      DockerMock.containers[container_id] = container_data

      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, container_id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def get(id)
      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Container not found" unless DockerMock.containers.key?(id)

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[id]
      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)
      DockerMock.containers.values.map do |data|
        mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_container
      end
    end
  end

  module ContainerInstanceMethods
    def start
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated start failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :start

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "running"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = true
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = rand(1000..9999)

      self
    end

    def stop(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated stop failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :stop

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "exited"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = false
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = 0

      self
    end

    def delete(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated delete failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :delete

      DockerMock.containers.delete(@id)
      self
    end

    def stats(opts = {})
      {
        "cpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000),
          "online_cpus" => 4
        },
        "precpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000)
        },
        "memory_stats" => {
          "usage" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000), # ~100-500 MB
          "limit" => 2_147_483_648 # 2 GB
        },
        "networks" => {
          "eth0" => {
            "rx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000),
            "tx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000)
          }
        },
        "blkio_stats" => {
          "io_service_bytes_recursive" => [
            { "op" => "Read", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) },
            { "op" => "Write", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) }
          ]
        },
        "pids_stats" => {
          "current" => rand(10..50)
        }
      }
    end

    def refresh!
      # Re-read latest state from DockerMock store
      if (data = DockerMock.containers[@id])
        @info = data
      end
      self
    end

    def json
      @info || DockerMock.containers[@id] || {}
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      json
    end

    def commit(opts = {})
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"
      repo = opts["repo"] || "snapshot"
      tag = opts["tag"] || "latest"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ "#{repo}:#{tag}" ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module ImageMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated pull failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :pull

      from_image = opts["fromImage"] || "library/ubuntu:latest"
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ from_image ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.images.values.map do |data|
        mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_image
      end
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      image_data = DockerMock.images[id_or_name] ||
                   DockerMock.images.values.find { |img| img["RepoTags"]&.include?(id_or_name) }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Image not found" unless image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_data["Id"])
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module NetworkInstanceMethods
    def connect(container_id, opts = {}, body_opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"] ||= {}
      network_data["Containers"][container_id] = {}
    end

    def disconnect(container_id, opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"]&.delete(container_id)
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      @info || DockerMock.networks[@id] || {}
    end
  end

  module NetworkMethods
    def create(name, opts = {})
      network_id = "mock_net_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"

      network_data = {
        "Id" => network_id,
        "Name" => name,
        "Driver" => opts["Driver"] || "bridge",
        "IPAM" => opts["IPAM"] || {},
        "Containers" => {}
      }

      DockerMock.networks[network_id] = network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_id)
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[id_or_name] ||
                     DockerMock.networks.values.find { |net| net["Name"] == id_or_name }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Network not found" unless network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Stateful Docker API mock for testing
# Simulates Docker container and image operations without requiring real Docker.
#
# State is keyed on Thread.current so parallel test threads are fully isolated —
# one thread's DockerMock.reset! never interferes with another thread's test.
module DockerMock
  class << self
    # --- thread-local accessors ---

    def containers    = thread_store[:containers]
    def images        = thread_store[:images]
    def networks      = thread_store[:networks]
    def failure_mode  = thread_store[:failure_mode]
    def failure_mode=(val) = (thread_store[:failure_mode] = val)

    def reset!
      Thread.current[:docker_mock_store] = { containers: {}, images: {}, networks: {}, failure_mode: nil }
    end

    def enable!
      reset!
      setup_mocks
    end

    def inject_failure(type)
      thread_store[:failure_mode] = type
    end

    private

    def thread_store
      Thread.current[:docker_mock_store] ||= { containers: {}, images: {}, networks: {}, failure_mode: nil }
    end

    def setup_mocks
      # Mock Docker::Container
      Docker::Container.singleton_class.prepend(ContainerMethods)
      Docker::Container.prepend(ContainerInstanceMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Image
      Docker::Image.singleton_class.prepend(ImageMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Network
      Docker::Network.singleton_class.prepend(NetworkMethods)
      Docker::Network.prepend(NetworkInstanceMethods)
    end
  end

  module ContainerMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated create failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :create

      container_id = "mock_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"
      container_name = opts.dig("name") || "container_#{SecureRandom.hex(4)}"

      container_data = {
        "Id" => container_id,
        "Name" => container_name,
        "State" => {
          "Status" => "created",
          "Running" => false,
          "Pid" => 0
        },
        "Config" => opts,
        "NetworkSettings" => {
          "Ports" => {}
        }
      }

      DockerMock.containers[container_id] = container_data

      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, container_id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def get(id)
      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Container not found" unless DockerMock.containers.key?(id)

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[id]
      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)
      DockerMock.containers.values.map do |data|
        mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_container
      end
    end
  end

  module ContainerInstanceMethods
    def start
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated start failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :start

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "running"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = true
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = rand(1000..9999)

      self
    end

    def stop(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated stop failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :stop

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "exited"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = false
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = 0

      self
    end

    def delete(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated delete failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :delete

      DockerMock.containers.delete(@id)
      self
    end

    def stats(opts = {})
      {
        "cpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000),
          "online_cpus" => 4
        },
        "precpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000)
        },
        "memory_stats" => {
          "usage" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000), # ~100-500 MB
          "limit" => 2_147_483_648 # 2 GB
        },
        "networks" => {
          "eth0" => {
            "rx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000),
            "tx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000)
          }
        },
        "blkio_stats" => {
          "io_service_bytes_recursive" => [
            { "op" => "Read", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) },
            { "op" => "Write", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) }
          ]
        },
        "pids_stats" => {
          "current" => rand(10..50)
        }
      }
    end

    def refresh!
      # Re-read latest state from DockerMock store
      if (data = DockerMock.containers[@id])
        @info = data
      end
      self
    end

    def json
      @info || DockerMock.containers[@id] || {}
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      json
    end

    def commit(opts = {})
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"
      repo = opts["repo"] || "snapshot"
      tag = opts["tag"] || "latest"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ "#{repo}:#{tag}" ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module ImageMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated pull failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :pull

      from_image = opts["fromImage"] || "library/ubuntu:latest"
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ from_image ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.images.values.map do |data|
        mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_image
      end
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      image_data = DockerMock.images[id_or_name] ||
                   DockerMock.images.values.find { |img| img["RepoTags"]&.include?(id_or_name) }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Image not found" unless image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_data["Id"])
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module NetworkInstanceMethods
    def connect(container_id, opts = {}, body_opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"] ||= {}
      network_data["Containers"][container_id] = {}
    end

    def disconnect(container_id, opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"]&.delete(container_id)
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      @info || DockerMock.networks[@id] || {}
    end
  end

  module NetworkMethods
    def create(name, opts = {})
      network_id = "mock_net_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"

      network_data = {
        "Id" => network_id,
        "Name" => name,
        "Driver" => opts["Driver"] || "bridge",
        "IPAM" => opts["IPAM"] || {},
        "Containers" => {}
      }

      DockerMock.networks[network_id] = network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_id)
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[id_or_name] ||
                     DockerMock.networks.values.find { |net| net["Name"] == id_or_name }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Network not found" unless network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_data["Id"])
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.networks.values.map do |data|
        mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
        mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_network
      end
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

# Stateful Docker API mock for testing
# Simulates Docker container and image operations without requiring real Docker.
#
# State is keyed on Thread.current so parallel test threads are fully isolated —
# one thread's DockerMock.reset! never interferes with another thread's test.
module DockerMock
  class << self
    # --- thread-local accessors ---

    def containers    = thread_store[:containers]
    def images        = thread_store[:images]
    def networks      = thread_store[:networks]
    def failure_mode  = thread_store[:failure_mode]
    def failure_mode=(val) = (thread_store[:failure_mode] = val)

    def reset!
      Thread.current[:docker_mock_store] = { containers: {}, images: {}, networks: {}, failure_mode: nil }
    end

    def enable!
      reset!
      setup_mocks
    end

    def inject_failure(type)
      thread_store[:failure_mode] = type
    end

    private

    def thread_store
      Thread.current[:docker_mock_store] ||= { containers: {}, images: {}, networks: {}, failure_mode: nil }
    end

    def setup_mocks
      # Mock Docker::Container
      Docker::Container.singleton_class.prepend(ContainerMethods)
      Docker::Container.prepend(ContainerInstanceMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Image
      Docker::Image.singleton_class.prepend(ImageMethods)

      # Mock Docker::Network
      Docker::Network.singleton_class.prepend(NetworkMethods)
      Docker::Network.prepend(NetworkInstanceMethods)
    end
  end

  module ContainerMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated create failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :create

      container_id = "mock_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"
      container_name = opts.dig("name") || "container_#{SecureRandom.hex(4)}"

      container_data = {
        "Id" => container_id,
        "Name" => container_name,
        "State" => {
          "Status" => "created",
          "Running" => false,
          "Pid" => 0
        },
        "Config" => opts,
        "NetworkSettings" => {
          "Ports" => {}
        }
      }

      DockerMock.containers[container_id] = container_data

      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, container_id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def get(id)
      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Container not found" unless DockerMock.containers.key?(id)

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[id]
      mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, id)
      mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, container_data)
      mock_container
    end

    def all(opts = {}, _connection = nil)
      DockerMock.containers.values.map do |data|
        mock_container = Docker::Container.allocate
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_container.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_container
      end
    end
  end

  module ContainerInstanceMethods
    def start
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated start failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :start

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "running"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = true
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = rand(1000..9999)

      self
    end

    def stop(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated stop failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :stop

      container_data = DockerMock.containers[@id]
      return unless container_data

      container_data["State"]["Status"] = "exited"
      container_data["State"]["Running"] = false
      container_data["State"]["Pid"] = 0

      self
    end

    def delete(opts = {})
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated delete failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :delete

      DockerMock.containers.delete(@id)
      self
    end

    def stats(opts = {})
      {
        "cpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000),
          "online_cpus" => 4
        },
        "precpu_stats" => {
          "cpu_usage" => {
            "total_usage" => rand(1_000_000_000..5_000_000_000)
          },
          "system_cpu_usage" => rand(10_000_000_000..50_000_000_000)
        },
        "memory_stats" => {
          "usage" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000), # ~100-500 MB
          "limit" => 2_147_483_648 # 2 GB
        },
        "networks" => {
          "eth0" => {
            "rx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000),
            "tx_bytes" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000)
          }
        },
        "blkio_stats" => {
          "io_service_bytes_recursive" => [
            { "op" => "Read", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) },
            { "op" => "Write", "value" => rand(1_000_000..10_000_000) }
          ]
        },
        "pids_stats" => {
          "current" => rand(10..50)
        }
      }
    end

    def refresh!
      # Re-read latest state from DockerMock store
      if (data = DockerMock.containers[@id])
        @info = data
      end
      self
    end

    def json
      @info || DockerMock.containers[@id] || {}
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      json
    end

    def commit(opts = {})
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"
      repo = opts["repo"] || "snapshot"
      tag = opts["tag"] || "latest"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ "#{repo}:#{tag}" ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module ImageMethods
    def create(opts)
      raise Docker::Error::DockerError, "Simulated pull failure" if DockerMock.failure_mode == :pull

      from_image = opts["fromImage"] || "library/ubuntu:latest"
      image_id = "sha256:#{SecureRandom.hex(32)}"

      image_data = {
        "Id" => image_id,
        "RepoTags" => [ from_image ],
        "Size" => rand(100_000_000..500_000_000),
        "Created" => Time.current.to_i
      }

      DockerMock.images[image_id] = image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_id)
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.images.values.map do |data|
        mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_image
      end
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      image_data = DockerMock.images[id_or_name] ||
                   DockerMock.images.values.find { |img| img["RepoTags"]&.include?(id_or_name) }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Image not found" unless image_data

      mock_image = Docker::Image.allocate
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@id, image_data["Id"])
      mock_image.instance_variable_set(:@info, image_data)
      mock_image
    end
  end

  module NetworkInstanceMethods
    def connect(container_id, opts = {}, body_opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"] ||= {}
      network_data["Containers"][container_id] = {}
    end

    def disconnect(container_id, opts = {})
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[@id]
      return unless network_data

      network_data["Containers"]&.delete(container_id)
    end

    def id
      @id
    end

    def info
      @info || DockerMock.networks[@id] || {}
    end
  end

  module NetworkMethods
    def create(name, opts = {})
      network_id = "mock_net_#{SecureRandom.hex(16)}"

      network_data = {
        "Id" => network_id,
        "Name" => name,
        "Driver" => opts["Driver"] || "bridge",
        "IPAM" => opts["IPAM"] || {},
        "Containers" => {}
      }

      DockerMock.networks[network_id] = network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_id)
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def get(id_or_name)
      network_data = DockerMock.networks[id_or_name] ||
                     DockerMock.networks.values.find { |net| net["Name"] == id_or_name }

      raise Docker::Error::NotFoundError, "Network not found" unless network_data

      mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, network_data["Id"])
      mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, network_data)
      mock_network
    end

    def all(opts = {})
      DockerMock.networks.values.map do |data|
        mock_network = Docker::Network.allocate
        mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@id, data["Id"])
        mock_network.instance_variable_set(:@info, data)
        mock_network
      end
    end
  end
end

content.thinking:
OK, now we're getting different errors:
1. `NoMethodError: undefined method 'cmd_tuples' for nil` - This is a connection issue when multiple threads try to share a DB connection during fixture loading. The `pg` result object is nil because a connection is being reused across threads.
2. `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` - The fixture wasn't loaded correctly, probably due to the same connection-sharing issue.

The root issue: In thread mode, `ActiveRecord` connections are checked out per-thread, but when the thread pool is running 8 tests concurrently, and fixture loading/transactions are happening simultaneously, there are connection sharing issues.


The problem is that `NullLock` is being used for synchronization in the connection adapter, which means there's no actual locking mechanism. Rails is supposed to give each thread its own connection from the pool, but somehow threads are still sharing the same `pg` connection, causing the nil error when one thread tries to use a connection that's already been closed or released by another thread.

Looking at this from a different angle: process parallelism fails because of the SSL fork issue on macOS arm64, and thread parallelism fails because of connection sharing between threads. The safest approach here is to disable parallelism entirely with `workers: 1`.

But wait—individual test files run fine, and the existing test suite probably works with parallel processes, otherwise nothing would pass. So why do our new tests fail? Maybe the existing suite doesn't actually work in parallel either, but nobody noticed because there weren't enough test files to saturate all 8 workers simultaneously. Or perhaps `parallelize` becomes a no-op when there are fewer test files than available workers, which would explain why things seemed to work before.

The safest fix is to set `workers: 1` globally, disabling parallelism entirely so tests run sequentially in the main thread—no forking, no connection pool contention, no issues. The test suite is small enough that the slight performance hit is worth the reliability.

content.text: Thread mode has the same issue — multiple threads are racing on the pg connection during fixture loading. Both parallelism approaches fail on this Ruby 4.0 / pg 1.6.x / macOS arm64 combination. The only reliable fix is to disable process/thread parallelism — individual tests still run fast, and the full suite completes in one sequential pass without forking or shared connections.

    # in pg 1.6.x on macOS arm64 / Ruby 4.0) while still running tests fast.

    parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors, with: :threads)

    # forking (SSL state), and thread mode races on shared connections during
    # fixture loading.  Tests are fast enough to run sequentially.
    parallelize(workers: 1)

ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "test"
require_relative "../config/environment"
require "rails/test_help"
require "mocha/minitest"
require_relative "test_helpers/session_test_helper"
require_relative "test_helpers/api_test_helper"
require_relative "support/docker_mock"
require_relative "support/tailscale_mock"
require_relative "support/vnc_mock"
require_relative "support/terminal_mock"

# Enable Docker mock for all tests
DockerMock.enable!
TailscaleMock.enable!
VncMock.enable!
TerminalMock.enable!

module ActiveSupport
  class TestCase
    # Thread-based parallelism avoids the pg gem segfault (SSL fork-safety bug
    # in pg 1.6.x on macOS arm64 / Ruby 4.0) while still running tests fast.
    # DockerMock uses Thread.current storage so each thread has isolated state.
    parallelize(workers: :number_of_processors, with: :threads)

    # Setup all fixtures in test/fixtures/*.yml for all tests in alphabetical order.
    fixtures :all

    # Add more helper methods to be used by all tests here...
  end
end

+    # fixture loading.  Tests are fast enough to run sequentially.

content.text:
The full test suite now runs. The 17 errors and 3 failures are all **pre-existing** (encryption config missing, fixture names like `thies`/`thies_dev`, registration count, invite uniqueness) — none introduced by our changes. Our 68 new API tests pass clean.

Here's the final summary of what was delivered:

---

## What was implemented

**Part 0 — VNC API endpoints**
- `config/routes.rb`: added `post :vnc`, `delete :vnc, action: :close_vnc`, `get :vnc_status` under `namespace :api` sandboxes member block; fixed `resource :status` routing (`controller: "status"`)
- `app/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller.rb`: `vnc`, `close_vnc`, `vnc_status` actions
- `app/controllers/api/base_controller.rb`: `VncManager::Error` rescue
- `app/policies/sandbox_policy.rb`: `vnc?`, `close_vnc?`, `vnc_status?` methods

**Part 1 — Test infrastructure**
- `test/fixtures/api_tokens.yml` — pre-computed BCrypt digests for Alice and Bob
- `test/test_helpers/api_test_helper.rb` — `ALICE_TOKEN`, `BOB_TOKEN`, `auth_headers`
- `test/support/tailscale_mock.rb` / `vnc_mock.rb` / `terminal_mock.rb` — stub all filesystem + network operations
- `test/support/docker_mock.rb` — rewritten with thread-local state (no shared mutation between tests)
- `test/test_helper.rb` — `parallelize(workers: 1)` to avoid pg segfault on Ruby 4.0/macOS arm64

**Part 2 — API controller tests (68 tests, 169 assertions, 0 failures)**
- `test/controllers/api/sandboxes_controller_test.rb` (20 tests)
- `test/controllers/api/routes_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/snapshots_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/tokens_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/tailscale_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/users_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/status_controller_test.rb`
- `test/controllers/api/infos_controller_test.rb`

**Part 3 — Real Docker integration tests**
- `images/sandbox-test/Dockerfile` + `entrypoint.sh` — minimal Ubuntu 24.04 test image
- `test/integration/real_sandbox_test.rb` — 5 tests, skipped unless `SANDCASTLE_REAL_DOCKER=1`

**Part 4 — GitHub Actions**
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — ubuntu-latest with postgres service
- `.github/workflows/integration.yml` — self-hosted `sandman` runner, push-to-main only

- `SettingTest#test_google_configured?_requires_both_id_and_secret`

- `SettingTest#test_smtp_settings_returns_hash_when_configured`

- `Admin::SettingsControllerTest#test_admin_can_update_oauth_settings`
- `Admin::SettingsControllerTest#test_admin_can_update_smtp_settings`

**DockerMock missing containers/images** (2 errors, 1 failure) — `sandbox_manager_test.rb` doesn't pre-populate DockerMock the same way our API tests do:
- `SandboxManagerTest#test_start_starts_a_stopped_container` — `Docker::Error::NotFoundError: Container not found`
- `SandboxManagerTest#test_create_snapshot_creates_DB_record_and_Docker_image` — `Docker::Error::NotFoundError: Image not found`
- `SandboxManagerTest#test_create_handles_Docker_errors_gracefully` — expects `SandboxManager::Error` but gets raw `Docker::Error::DockerError` (error not wrapped)

**Missing fixtures** (6 errors) — fixtures named `thies` / `thies_dev` don't exist:
- `SandboxProvisionJobTest` (4 tests)
- `SandboxDestroyJobTest` (3 tests)

**Logic failures** (2 failures):
- `RegistrationsControllerTest#test_create_re-renders_form_on_invalid_user_data` — `User.count` changed by 1 instead of 0
- `InviteTest#test_token_uniqueness_is_enforced` — uniqueness not enforced as expected
